overallSummary: Fabio Giglietto maintains a strong and highly targeted professional presence across Bluesky, LinkedIn, and Mastodon, focusing on computational social science, disinformation, and coordinated behavior. With a combined following of 3,580, his content generates consistent and high-quality engagement, particularly when announcing major research milestones, publications, and academic opportunities.

contentThemes.description: Research and methodologies related to detecting coordinated link sharing and information operations, including tools like CooRnet.
Announcements regarding research grants, hiring postdoctoral researchers, and PhD opportunities in computational social science.
Participation in conferences (like DisinfoConf2025) and discussions surrounding research methodology.

recommendations: Incorporate visual elements like diagrams, data visualizations, or screenshots of research tools (e.g., CooRnet v2.0) to boost visual engagement across all platforms.
Leverage LinkedIn's professional network more actively for recruitment by encouraging existing connections to share postdoc and PhD opportunities.
Increase posting frequency on Mastodon to build stronger community engagement, focusing on technical and methodological discussions.
Use more platform-specific hashtags, especially on Bluesky and LinkedIn, to reach wider audiences interested in computational social science.

platformInsights.bluesky: Bluesky currently drives the highest average engagement rate (6.14%), making it an excellent platform for immediate peer feedback, academic networking, and sharing newly published papers.
platformInsights.mastodon: Mastodon provides a dedicated niche audience with stable engagement (4.55%), particularly receptive to open-source tool updates like CooRnet, though overall reach is smaller.
platformInsights.linkedin: LinkedIn has the largest audience (1,850 followers) and drives the highest absolute engagement numbers. It performs exceptionally well for high-level announcements such as grants and job openings.

topPerformingContent.content: Just published our new paper on coordinated inauthentic behavior on social media platforms!
New research opportunity for PhD students interested in computational social science. Reply for details!
Excited to announce that our research team has been awarded a new grant to study information operations across social media platforms.
